summary: See clues file for full details. 13 tossups, 17 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 13 tossups and 17 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: Two brothers with this surname who championed Esperanto as a literary language were named Reto and Cezaro.
examples: Two brothers with this surname who championed Esperanto as a literary language were named Reto and Cezaro.

clues.clue: A poem by an author with this surname that consists of a series of one-line questions and answers asks, "Will there be beds for me and all who seek?" An author with this surname opened a collection by calling a sonnet "a moment's monument." The poem "Up-Hill" is by an author with this surname who also wrote that "One may lead a horse to water, Twenty cannot make him drink." The sonnet sequence The House of Life is by an author with this surname who wrote "The Blessed Damozel" and illustrated his sister's poem about Laura and Lizzie.
examples: A poem by an author with this surname that consists of a series of one-line questions and answers asks, "Will there be beds for me and all who seek?" An author with this surname opened a collection by calling a sonnet "a moment's monument." The poem "Up-Hill" is by an author with this surname who also wrote that "One may lead a horse to water, Twenty cannot make him drink." The sonnet sequence The House of Life is by an author with this surname who wrote "The Blessed Damozel" and illustrated his sister's poem about Laura and Lizzie.

clues.clue: The narrator of a dramatic monologue about a "lazy laughing languid" character places gold coins into this substance.
examples: The narrator of a dramatic monologue about a "lazy laughing languid" character places gold coins into this substance.

clues.clue: This substance was the "first gold" according to the sonnet "Body's Beauty," which describes it surrounding Adam's heart.
examples: This substance was the "first gold" according to the sonnet "Body's Beauty," which describes it surrounding Adam's heart.

clues.clue: An author trying to recover a manuscript supposedly found that this substance had filled Elizabeth Siddal's coffin after her death, an event later linked to a poem that describes seven stars in this substance.
examples: An author trying to recover a manuscript supposedly found that this substance had filled Elizabeth Siddal's coffin after her death, an event later linked to a poem that describes seven stars in this substance.

clues.clue: Lacking money, (*) Laura trades a "tear more rare than pearl" and this substance for fruit in "Goblin Market." This substance is made into a constellation at the end of a poem in which a Sylph is sliced in half trying to protect it from the Baron.
examples: Lacking money, (*) Laura trades a "tear more rare than pearl" and this substance for fruit in "Goblin Market." This substance is made into a constellation at the end of a poem in which a Sylph is sliced in half trying to protect it from the Baron.

clues.clue: A painting by this artist includes white roses, poppies, and foxgloves around a woman who gazes into a hand mirror as she brushes "enchanted hair." That painting by this artist, Lady Lilith, was paired with a painting of a woman pensively holding a violin bow and titled Veronica Veronese.
examples: A painting by this artist includes white roses, poppies, and foxgloves around a woman who gazes into a hand mirror as she brushes "enchanted hair." That painting by this artist, Lady Lilith, was paired with a painting of a woman pensively holding a violin bow and titled Veronica Veronese.

clues.clue: Those works hung in the same room as a painting by this artist that depicted his patron's wife, Frances, as Monna Rosa arranging pink roses in a porcelain vase.
examples: Those works hung in the same room as a painting by this artist that depicted his patron's wife, Frances, as Monna Rosa arranging pink roses in a porcelain vase.

clues.clue: Those three paintings made up half of the six "stunners" that this artist painted for the drawing room of Frederick Leyland, which also included a painting by this man in which a woman with seven stars in her hair holds three lilies in her arms.
examples: Those three paintings made up half of the six "stunners" that this artist painted for the drawing room of Frederick Leyland, which also included a painting by this man in which a woman with seven stars in her hair holds three lilies in her arms.

clues.clue: This artist, who frequently employed the model Alexa Wilding, inscribed the frame of that painting with a poem describing eyes "deeper than the depth / of waters stilled at even" and hair "yellow like ripe corn." For 10 points, name this Pre-Raphaelite who painted The Blessed Damozel.
examples: This artist, who frequently employed the model Alexa Wilding, inscribed the frame of that painting with a poem describing eyes "deeper than the depth / of waters stilled at even" and hair "yellow like ripe corn." For 10 points, name this Pre-Raphaelite who painted The Blessed Damozel.

clues.clue: One author with this surname wrote a poem whose guilty speaker addresses a man whose "eyes look earthward" and notes "There's blood between us, love" and remembers how "You sinned with me a pleasant (+) sin".
examples: One author with this surname wrote a poem whose guilty speaker addresses a man whose "eyes look earthward" and notes "There's blood between us, love" and remembers how "You sinned with me a pleasant (+) sin".

clues.clue: The speaker of another poem by that author with this surname asks the reader to "tell me of our future that you plann'd" before asking them not to "Remember and be sad".
examples: The speaker of another poem by that author with this surname asks the reader to "tell me of our future that you plann'd" before asking them not to "Remember and be sad".
